steps.title: Translate the notation
steps.content: $f(2)$ means the $y$-value on the graph when $x=2$. So this is a graph-reading question: go to $x=2$ and read the height of the curve.

steps.title: Read the graph at x = 2
steps.content: At $x=2$, the curve is on the $x$-axis. That point is $(2,0)$, so the output value is $f(2)=0$.

steps.title: Check the choices
steps.content: The value $0$ appears in choice D, so choice D is correct.
